speaker: RICHARD
text: Now, my Lord Hastings and Sir William Stanley,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Leave off to wonder why I drew you hither

speaker: RICHARD
text: Into this chiefest thicket of the park.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thus stands the case: you know our king, my brother,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is prisoner to the Bishop here, at whose hands

speaker: RICHARD
text: He hath good usage and great liberty,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And, often but attended with weak guard,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Comes hunting this way to disport himself.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I have advertised him by secret means

speaker: RICHARD
text: That, if about this hour he make this way

speaker: RICHARD
text: Under the color of his usual game,

speaker: RICHARD
text: He shall here find his friends with horse and men

speaker: RICHARD
text: To set him free from his captivity.

speaker: HUNTSMAN
text: This way, my lord, for this way lies the game.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Nay, this way, man. See where the huntsmen stand.—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Now, brother of Gloucester, Lord Hastings, and the rest,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Stand you thus close to steal the Bishop’s deer?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Brother, the time and case requireth haste.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your horse stands ready at the park corner.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: But whither shall we then?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: To Lynn, my lord, and shipped from thence to Flanders.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Well guessed, believe me, for that was my meaning.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Stanley, I will requite thy forwardness.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But wherefore stay we? ’Tis no time to talk.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Huntsman, what sayst thou? Wilt thou go along?

speaker: HUNTSMAN
text: Better do so than tarry and be hanged.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Come then, away! Let’s ha’ no more ado.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Bishop, farewell; shield thee from Warwick’s frown,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And pray that I may repossess the crown.
